key_issues: No blocking issues. All acceptance criteria met; 86 checks pass with no FAILURE/ERROR.
C1 verified: json-object? is a local private define (errors.rkt:202), NOT imported from spec-2025-11-25.rkt; require list unchanged.
Data-gating is real and correct: both conjuncts of each gate are checked; wrong-shape data falls to the generic else carrying the received code, no throw.

NOTE (non-blocking): because specialized and generic branches produce identical output today, the C2/C3 tests pin the gate predicate's shape but cannot fail a gate-OMITTING impl by observable behavior (only a mis-gated one). This is the intended struct-collapse design, acknowledged in the code comments, not a test defect.
Round-trip (code/message/data preserved), reverse fixpoint (specialized + generic), subtype erasure (base/auth -> protocol), message-verbatim, and data-by-reference all asserted.
